home.title: Ashu Devgune — School of Indian Classical Dance

home.lede: A small studio for Bharatanatyam, Folk dance and allied forms — rooted in tradition, open to anyone willing to begin.
home.intro_body_1: Indian classical dance is less about performance than it is about listening — to a rhythmic cycle, to a teacher, to the weight of your own body. The school teaches the foundations carefully and unhurriedly, from first footwork (tatkar) through storytelling (abhinaya).
home.intro_body_2: Classes are open to beginners from age seven, with separate groups for adults. No prior experience is required — only the willingness to return each week.
home.intro_pull: To dance is to keep time with something older than yourself.
home.d1_desc: Regional folk traditions from Maharashtra and Rajasthan — lavani, koli, ghoomar and kalbelia. Earthy rhythm, group formations and the characteristic footwork of each region. Bollywood is offered as a separate course.
home.d2_desc: The codified South Indian form, built on adavus and the geometry of the body. Structure first, expression second — then, eventually, both at once.
home.d3_desc: A gentler introduction for ages 7–12 — posture, rhythm, simple stories. No exam pressure; the aim is a lifelong relationship with the art.
home.visit_heading: New students are welcomed each term.
home.visit_body: Drop in for a trial class before committing. The studio is small on purpose — we ask that you write ahead.

about.lede: Asharani (Ashu) Devgune is a professional dancer, choreographer and teacher with decades of experience in Indian classical and folk traditions.
about.body_1: Originally from Pune, Maharashtra, she is a Bachelor of Performing Arts in Bharatanatyam (University of Pune), with additional training through Prayag Sangeet Samiti and Nrutya Zankar Sangeet Academy.
about.body_2: Her core practice includes Bharatanatyam, Indian folk dance and Bollywood. She has taught and performed across India, Europe and Africa, including founding the dance school Nupur Nrutyala in Tanzania.
about.body_3: With more than 2,000 performances and years of choreography, workshops and productions, Ashu leads the school with warmth, discipline and a commitment to helping every student grow with confidence.
about.pull: The form is not mine to change. It is mine to pass on, as carefully as I can.
about.lineage_body: Raised in Pune, a city known for its artistic heritage, Ashu brings a training culture that balances precision, expression and respect for tradition in every class.
about.credentials_1: Bachelor of Performing Arts (Bharatanatyam) — University of Pune, India
about.credentials_3: Former dancer and choreographer for Sumit and T-Series in India
about.credentials_4: 2,000+ performances, plus workshops and choreography across India, Europe and Africa; featured in the STAR Gent artist list for the past 6 years

classes.lede: Term runs from September through June, mirroring the Belgian school calendar. A full schedule is below; please write to confirm a place before your first class.
classes.intro: Every student begins with a fifteen-minute conversation — about why you are here, what you have studied, and what you hope for. From there we agree on the right group.
classes.d1_desc: A living survey of two regional traditions. Maharashtrian forms — lavani, koli, dindi — alongside the Rajasthani ghoomar and kalbelia. Beginners start with stance, rhythm and simple steps; intermediate and advanced groups build toward full sequences with prop and costume conventions. Bollywood is taught as a separate course and is not part of this curriculum.
classes.d2_desc: Adavus, the ten basic stances, and a slow accumulation of jathis. Theory of rhythm (tala) is taught alongside the movement from the outset.
classes.d3_desc: A playful, unhurried introduction. Posture, rhythm, short stories drawn from epics. Small group so that each child can be seen and corrected kindly.
classes.sch_note: Subject to term dates. Belgian school holidays observed.
classes.pricing_body: Monthly subscription, billed per trimester. A single trial class is offered at €[XX] and deducted from your enrolment if you continue. Reduced rates for second siblings. Full pricing on request.

gallery.lede: Images from classes, studio rehearsals and the 2019 winter recital. A fuller video archive is on the way.
gallery.video_body: Recital clips will be added here shortly. Subscribe on YouTube to be notified.

contact.lede: The quickest way to join is to send a short note — your name, age group, any previous training, and which city is closer. You will hear back within a few days.
contact.form_note: The form is not yet connected — please write directly to the email above in the meantime.
